author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
mahābhāva⋅cintāmaṇi, udbhāvita tanukhāni,
sakhīpati⋅sajja⋅prabhāvatī
kāruṇya⋅tāruṇya āra, lāvaṇya⋅amṛtadhāra,
tāhe snātā lakṣmī-jayī satī

lajjā paṭṭa-vastra jā’ra, saundarya kuṅkuma⋅sāra,
kasturī⋅citrita kalevara
kampāśru⋅pulaka⋅raṅga, stambha⋅sveda⋅svarabhaṅga,
jāḍyānmāda⋅navaratnadhara

pañcaviṁśatika⋅guṇa⋅, phulamālā suśobhana,
dhīrādhīrā⋅bhāva⋅paṭṭavāsā
pihita⋅māna⋅dhammillā, saubhāgya⋅tilakojjvalā,
kṛṣṇa⋅nāma⋅yaśaḥ⋅karṇollāsā

rāgatāmbūlita⋅oṣṭha, kauṭilya⋅kajjala spaṣṭa,
smitakarpūrita⋅narmaśīlā
kīrtiyaśa⋅antaḥpure, garva⋅khaṭṭopari sphure,
dulita⋅premavaicittya⋅mālā

praṇayaroṣa⋅kañculī⋅, pihita⋅stanayugmakā,
candrājayī kacchapī⋅raviṇī
sakhīdvayaskandhe līlā⋅, karāmbujārpaṇaśīlā,
śyāmā śyāmāmṛta⋅vitaraṇī

e hena rādhikā⋅pada, tomādera susampada,
dante tṛṇa jāce tava pāya
e bhaktivinoda dīna, rādhā⋅dāsyāmṛta⋅kaṇa,
rūpa raghunātha! deha tāya
en_translation:
She is a jewel of the highest ecstatic love, whose transcendental form is manifested, glorious, and adorned as the chief of the companions. She is a stream of nectar of compassion, youth, and beauty, in which she—the chaste lady who conquers the goddess of fortune—is bathed.

Her silken garment is shyness, her beauty is the essence of saffron, and her body is adorned with musk. Trembling, tears, thrills of ecstasy, paralysis, perspiration, a faltering voice, and madness are her nine jewels.

Adorned with a beautiful flower garland of twenty-five qualities, her silken dress is a mood of both patience and impatience. Her braided hair conceals her pique, she shines with the mark of good fortune, and the glory of Kṛṣṇa’s names is the delight of her ears.

Her lips are red with the betel of attachment, her clear eyeliner is her charming crookedness, and she is playful, perfumed with the camphor of smiles. In the inner chambers of fame and glory, she shines upon the throne of pride, wearing the swaying garland of the uniqueness of love.

Her blouse of loving anger covers her breasts; she conquers the moon and is the melody of the lute. Playfully placing her lotus hands on the shoulders of two companions, she distributes the nectar of Kṛṣṇa.

Such feet of Rādhikā are your great wealth; with a blade of grass in my teeth, I beg at your feet. This humble Bhaktivinoda asks for a particle of the nectar of service to Rādhā. O Rūpa, O Raghunātha! Please give it to him.
